Order To Show Cause Why Temporary Injunction Should Not Be Ordered
Off Calendar May-03-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Tyler Rougeau, Esq. (415) 771-9850 x 1116 tyler@thclinic.org of Tenderloin Housing Clinic for Plaintiff.
Allen Thomas Koster III, Esq. (415) 680 0023 A.Thomas.Koster@gmail.com for Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defense Counsel argues the proposed preliminary injunction is vague, no basis for the injunction based on the complaint, some Defendants have moved out of the property, argues the new mailbox, damages incurred everyday and a requirement for posting a bond. The Court shares its views. Plaintiff responds. The Court asks specific issues. Defense Counsel responds. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Order To Show Cause Why Temporary Injunction Should Not Be Ordered/Plaintiff's Motion for a Preliminary Injunction.

Taken Under Submission. Defense Counsel to submit a declaration with attachments targeting damages on 4/13/21 by 12 noon to Department210@sftc.org

Judge: Vedica Puri, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. =(210/VP)
